hero.lede: A Bali football club competing at regional level — built by Balinese and internationals who travelled here to wear the green. Spin the ball. Find the squad.
hero.globeHint: Drag to spin · click a country to meet the players

club.title:
Family,
built to compete.
club.kicker: A football club with regional ambition. Family as foundation. Standards as the gate.
club.pull:
We didn't get scouted.
We got here.
club.para1: Ubud Jungle Ballers is a football club with a clear ambition: to become Ubud's largest club and one of the most respected amateur sides in Bali. We're building a world-class amateur ecosystem — competitive at regional level, professionally structured, and deeply Balinese.
club.para2: Some of us were born five minutes from the pitch. Some flew sixteen hours and never went back. The green doesn't ask where you're from — only if you'll run for the player next to you.
club.tenet1Body: Adapt. Watch. Arrive grounded — the jungle teaches you how to play.
club.tenet2Body: Equal seats, one shirt. Balinese leadership. Open doors.
club.tenet3Body: A family without structure stagnates. Show up. Train honestly. Earn the green.
club.alt1: The Ubud Jungle Ballers squad together after a session
club.alt2: Players in yellow bibs during a night training match
club.alt3: A player dribbling past a defender on a futsal court
club.alt4: Teammates relaxing on the steps after training

community.title:
The pitch is
open.
community.kicker: The non-negotiable rhythm. Show up to one. Show up to all four. Bring boots.
community.seoLine: Open football, futsal and mini-soccer sessions in Ubud and the Gianyar area — almost every day of the week, for all levels, with internationals and locals welcome.

sessions.title: First Team training
sessions.body: Tactical work, set pieces, match prep for the regional league.

sessions.title: Second Team training
sessions.body: Skills, game scenarios, conditioning. The bridge to Tier 01.

sessions.title: Fun First — community
sessions.body: Open to all members. Mixed levels, mixed pace, futsal/mini-soccer.

sessions.title: First Team — mandatory
sessions.body: Sharper. Faster. Where match-day selection is decided.

sessions.title: WhatsApp main channel
sessions.body: Sign-ups, venue calls, match selection, ride-shares, banter.

sessions.title: Regional fixtures
sessions.body: League and cup. Announced in WhatsApp 48 hours ahead.

squad.title:
Three teams.
One standard.
squad.kicker: From open community sessions to a competitive first team chasing Bali regional honours.

partners.title: Stand with the green.
partners.kicker: Local businesses, regional partners. Want your name on the shirt? Talk to us.

joinCta.title:
Join our
club.
joinCta.body: The community already lives in WhatsApp. Sign-ups, venue calls, ride-shares, the daily small talk that keeps a club honest. New members get added to the right group within 24 hours.
joinCta.check1: Pick a session you want to play.
joinCta.fineprint: Or DM us on Instagram @ubudjungleballers

faq.title: Good to know.
faq.kicker: Football, futsal & mini-soccer in Ubud — answered.

items.q: Where can I play football in Ubud?
items.a: Ubud Jungle Ballers runs open football sessions in central Ubud almost every day of the week — weekday trainings plus social games. Pick a session, bring boots, and show up; new players are always welcome.

items.q: Do you play futsal or mini-soccer too?
items.a: Yes. Our Thursday "Fun First" session is a mixed-level, mixed-pace community game with futsal and mini-soccer, alongside full-pitch football across the rest of the week.

items.q: Can foreigners and expats join, or is it just for locals?
items.a: Both. The club is built by Balinese locals and internationals living in or visiting Bali. All nationalities are welcome — locals, expats and travellers alike.

items.q: I'm not very experienced — can I still play?
items.a: Absolutely. We run everything from casual, just-for-fun games to a competitive first team. Beginners and returning players are welcome at the open community sessions.

items.q: Do you play in the Gianyar and wider Bali area?
items.a: Yes. We're based in Ubud, in the Gianyar regency, and play regional fixtures around Bali — league and cup matches announced each week.

items.q: How much does it cost to join?
items.a: Joining the community is free. Sign up through the join form or message us on WhatsApp, and you'll be added to the right group within 24 hours.
Sessions are around 7 PM. First Team trains Monday and Friday, Second Team on Wednesday, the open "Fun First" community game is Thursday, and regional fixtures are on weekends.

items.q: Can women and girls join too?
items.a: Yes — Ubud Jungle Ballers is open to everyone. Women are part of our community and welcome at the open sessions. Come down, bring boots, and join in.

footer.tagline: A football club from Ubud, Bali. Open to everyone who'll run for the player next to them — competitive at regional level.

tour.heroSub: One ball, one crown. Gather your team and conquer the jungle at Liga Raya, Gianyar.
tour.prizesLead: Win through the groups and the knockouts — IDR 3,000,000 goes to the champions. Only one team lifts the Jungle Kings Cup.
tour.tpartnersLead: Jungle Kings Cup is powered by local partners who make the day happen.
tour.formatLead: Open entry, two groups, one long day of football at Liga Raya, Gianyar — from 3 PM kick-offs through the semis, the final, and an after-party to close it out. Here's the shape of the day.
tour.cDateNote: 3 PM first kick-off, football till 10 PM — then the after-party.
tour.cVenueNote: Liga Raya, Gianyar. View on map →
tour.cFormatNote: Open entry · two groups · semis, 3rd-place play-off & final.
tour.cEntryNote: per person · foreigner / local · refs, group games & the knockout run.
fmtList: Open entry · two groupsTeams drawn into groups — format scales with entries
Group stage: play each rival twiceDouble round-robin · 2 × 10 min games
Top two in each group advanceStraight into the semi-finals
Knockouts: 2 × 15 minSemis, a 3rd-place play-off, then the final
knowList: 7-a-sideSeven out on the pitch, roll your subs freely
Bring your own jerseyEvery team needs its own matching kit
Qualified refereesEvery match is properly officiated
Entry per person— foreigner / — local
tour.payLead: Spots are confirmed in order of payment received. Register below, then transfer the entry fee — your team is in once we see it.
tour.payDeadline: Open entry — first paid, first in. Pay by 1 July 2026 to confirm your place.
tour.payHint: After transferring, send your payment screenshot on WhatsApp to the club line with your team name. We'll confirm your spot within 24 hours.
tour.bandBody: Two minutes to register. Team name, your crest, a contact, and squad size — that's it.
tour.regEyebrowProgress: REGISTER A TEAM · STEP 1 / 3
tour.s1Sub: Name it, then drop your crest. This is how you'll show up on the bracket.
tour.logoHint: PNG, JPG or WEBP · up to 4 MB
tour.s2Sub: One contact per team. We'll send the venue, kickoff times and fixtures here.
tour.s3Sub: 7 on the pitch, up to 5 subs. Roughly how many players are you bringing?
tour.s3Note: Final player names aren't needed now — bring your squad on the day. Entry is — per foreigner / — per local, due on sign-up.
tour.doneSub: Your team is in. Pay the entry fee now to lock your spot — bank details below — then join the WhatsApp group to confirm.
tour.doneGroupCta: Join the Jungle Kings Cup WhatsApp group →
tour.doneRevoke: Not paid within 24 hours of sign-up? Your spot is released to the next team.
tour.errLogo: Add your team crest — any image works.
tour.errLogoType: That file isn't supported — use a PNG, JPG or WEBP.
tour.errLogoSize: Logo is over 4 MB — try a smaller file.
tour.errAdmin: Who's the captain? Pop a name in.
tour.errRoster: Squad size: a whole number between 7 and 20.
tour.errTimeout: That took too long — check your connection and tap Register again.
tour.errGeneric: Couldn't register that — {msg}. Your details are safe; tap Register again.
tour.doneMsgNamed: {name}, your team is in. Pay the entry fee now to lock your spot — bank details below — then join the WhatsApp group to confirm.
tour.doneMsgAnon: Your team is in. Pay the entry fee now to lock your spot — bank details below — then join the WhatsApp group to confirm.
